generated_note: JSON is canonical; docs/governance/registry.md is its rendering. Source documents win over this registry, always.

entries.title: NNNN — Decision Title
entries.role: Template for new decision records.

entries.title: 0001 — Local-first by default, hosted-compatible via user-held keys
entries.role: Fixes key custody: local-first by default, hosted-compatible via user-held keys; host treated as untrusted infrastructure; defines processing disclosure events.

entries.title: 0002 — Safety Surfacing Doctrine
entries.role: Defines the safety surfacing doctrine: speech-integrity model, four doctrines, intensity ladder, language law.

entries.title: W2 Process Decision — Relay Landing Ceremony Tiers
entries.role: Defines the relay landing ceremony tiers (Mechanical / Judgment / Fence-crossing), the anchor and commit-boundary rules, review vocabulary, standing erratum permission, sequencing latitude, non-negotiables, and the suggestion-forever clause; extends checklist rule 9.

entries.title: 0004 — Plaintext Residue Policy
entries.role: Fixes plaintext residue doctrine: default-deny persistence of decrypted content past task end; plaintext-free logs in every build configuration; enumerated forbidden artifact classes; exceptions only by decision record; platform residue documented, never denied.

entries.title: 0005 — Vault Encryption Stack Doctrine
entries.role: Fixes vault cryptography doctrine: no custom cryptography; authenticated encryption only; memory-hard key derivation; versioned format with escrow-free user-side migration; keychain integration deferred; final library selection deferred to spike evidence.

entries.title: 0006 — Runtime / Platform Stack Doctrine
entries.role: Fixes platform doctrine and the evaluation spike plan: verifiably telemetry-silent by observation, no background processes, user-initiated updates, accessibility as scored criterion; spike disposable and outside the repo with output hygiene rules; stack selection deferred to a final-selection record with evidence attached.

entries.title: 0007 — Development-Artifact Policy
entries.role: Fixes development-artifact law from the first runnable artifact: synthetic-only everywhere including local debugging; paste-is-publication; spike output hygiene with no machine-identifying detail beyond OS class; spike artifacts never become product artifacts; crash and debug artifacts local and human-shared only.

entries.title: 0008 — Runtime Stack — Final Selection
entries.role: Selects the engine spine's runtime and crypto binding on first-pass spike evidence: the local-process class (Python 3 with the PyNaCl libsodium binding); rejects the bundled-runtime class for the spine while leaving future surface reconsideration open; defers the native-shell class to the review-package phase; states Windows-class single-OS sufficiency for spine selection; binds the key-handling caveat and its W3-D2 mitigations; the first binding installation remains a future fence-crossing not authorised by this record.

entries.title: 0009 — Import File Boundary
entries.role: Fixes the import boundary (edge E1): bytes plus user-supplied provenance only; zero content interpretation at import; integrity inspection capped at type verification; provisional v1 format list hardening at the engine brief; anything beyond the boundary is processing under grant.

entries.title: 0010 — Minimal Review Posture
entries.role: Fixes the minimal review posture: tests simulate review via structurally unreachable doubles; nothing real becomes Approved before real review surfaces exist; the early caller supports rights-operation categories with review/approve explicitly excluded; no Approved-status item in any non-test store; reversal only by new record.

entries.title: 0011 — Local Backup Guidance
entries.role: Fixes the backup story without escrow: ciphertext backup may travel anywhere, the key travels nowhere; one-action encrypted export with symmetric user-performed restore; offered once beside the key-loss sentence, one restatement on skip, never nagged; no Wing-operated destination, no automatic backup, no background sync; recovery codes deferred to their own record.

entries.title: 0012 — Key-Loss Onboarding Wording
entries.role: Fixes the vetted key-loss sentence (the Wing's own inability named explicitly) and its doctrine: before vault creation, one deliberate acknowledgment, plain-not-frightening and never softened into false reassurance; said once then discoverable, never pushed; wording governed because the meaning is the wording; completion-rate concerns never drive softer language.

entries.title: 0013 — Key Derivation, Custody, and Envelope Selection
entries.role: Closes ADR 0005 named opens and fixes the v1 key architecture: moderate KDF profile as evidence-based review-dated provisional (named profiles only, parameters and salt recorded in the versioned key envelope); passphrase-alone custody with no escrow or recovery path in any form; two-layer wrap (records under a random master key, passphrase-derived key seals only the master); per-record sealed envelopes with cardinality/size leakage named for the threat register; the sealed key envelope travels with backups as ciphertext, restore requiring the passphrase; envelope resides in its own small versioned file. Authorises no implementation.

entries.title: 0014 — Licence Selection
entries.role: Selects the Apache License 2.0 for v1, standard and unmodified, with the public repository owner as copyright holder: broad reuse (commercial and closed-source included) is intentional; copyleft rejected as mission-fit not principle; custom and moral-use licensing rejected firmly; NOTICE is an informational redistribution notice carrying the copyright line, fork non-endorsement, and public-safety boundary statements without adding licence terms; governance intent lives in documentation; forks inherit code, never endorsement or certification. Project-level decision, not an engine-track deliverable.

entries.title: 0015 — Durable Ledger Doctrine
entries.role: Decides that the durable ledger exists and how: sealed under the master-key custody boundary because governance metadata is privacy-sensitive by pattern; independently sealed append frames as the doctrine direction; event scope doctrine-wide with v1 implementation limited to existing transition events (import/custody emission a named future extension, not mandated); the ledger joins the backup unit as sealed ciphertext; erasure is the user knowing act only, whole-ledger in v1, never a side effect of profile erasure; append-only with torn-tail one-event blast radius honestly stated. Sequences a small dedicated implementation milestone before the D6 brief. Authorises no implementation.

entries.title: 0016 — Room Contract Template
entries.role: Fixes the room-contract template: eleven required sections in fixed order and numbering, each present and non-blank, 'not applicable' written with a reason, a mandatory eleventh open-questions section reading 'None at acceptance.' when empty, and the Meditation reads-nothing case as the worked N/A example (no bespoke exception). Decides the skeleton only; fills no room's sections. Gates the four room contracts (W4-D2 through W4-D5) and the contract-conformance validator (W4-D6). Wording sections route to the future W6 governed string catalogue as review-of-record. Authorises no scaffolding and no implementation.

entries.title: 0017 — Room Isolation Model
entries.role: Sets the room-isolation standard that fills every room contract's Processing Boundary section: architectural isolation as doctrine — each room's processing interaction occurs in a context that has never contained another room's content; one processing grant binds exactly one room and the context derives from the grant; no interface accepts content from two rooms; cross-room content is unreachable, not merely forbidden; filtering or output controls alone are insufficient; grant scoping and isolation are separate complementary walls; W5 must later realise the property while W4 specifies no mechanism; behavioural evaluations still apply; any future exception requires its own accepted record with fixed minimum contents and no silent downgrade to policy isolation; the Meditation Room receives the uniform standard while its no-outward-signal property is left to DR-W4-04. The display-vs-processing-context split and per-room context-cost evidence are deferred to W5. Authorises no implementation.

entries.title: Governance Registry (machine-readable manifest)
entries.role: The canonical machine-readable governance registry manifest. Indexes authority; must not mint authority.

entries.title: Governance Registry (human-readable index)
entries.role: Human-readable rendering of the governance registry; carries the registry doctrine, limits, and authority rules. JSON is canonical; this file is its rendering.

entries.title: W0 — Wellbeing Wing Constitution
entries.role: The governing constitution: purpose, non-goals, core laws, room boundaries, the Health Vault pattern, failure modes, and open questions.

errata.note: Non-semantic header correction during W2-D2 landing, authorised by human reviewer: status line updated from 'Draft for review' to 'Accepted by human reviewer, 2026-06-12', aligning the source header with the acceptance sealed in W2-D1 section 2 and docs/phases/README.md. No other content changed.

entries.title: W1-D1 — Data Boundary Map
entries.role: Defines trust zones, sensitivity classes, homes, the category inventory, and the default-deny flow-edge whitelist.

entries.title: W1-D2 — Consent & Scope Model
entries.role: Defines the consent grammar: grant elements, grant types, anti-blanket rules, disclosure language, revocation immediate effects.

entries.title: W1-D3 — Authority & Staleness Model
entries.role: Defines the authority and staleness grammar: labels, transitions, contradiction handling, room consumption rules.

entries.title: W1-D5 — Threat Model
entries.role: Names the protected assets, trust boundaries, governance threat catalogue with mitigations, and open residual risks.

entries.title: W1-D6 — Evaluation Plan Skeleton
entries.role: Defines the evaluation plan skeleton: principles, deterministic and behavioural test categories, required failure examples, W1 closure criteria.

entries.title: W2 — Governance Evaluation & Enforcement Foundations (Phase Alignment Report)
entries.role: The W2 phase runway: A-D decision, fences, deliverable plan for governance evaluation and enforcement foundations. Authorises future W2 briefs, not immediate scaffolding.

entries.title: W2 Closure Record
entries.role: Seals W2: accepted deliverables with commit references, closure criteria assessment, verification summary, incident and learning log, deferred items, and the W3 phase gate. Authorises nothing to be built.

entries.title: W2-D1 — W1 Closure Record
entries.role: Seals W1: accepted corpus list, established baseline, carried-forward open decisions, W2 authority boundary. Authorises nothing to be built.

entries.title: W2-D2 — Governance Registry / Accepted Document Index (Alignment Brief)
entries.role: The accepted alignment brief for the governance registry: format, fields, directory scope, authority and conflict rules, hash rules.

entries.title: Phase Entry Checklist
entries.role: The repo governance checklist: status authority, two-tier change rule, registry atomicity, directory and root-file fences, dependency fence, public-safety rule, D5 definition fences, phase entry gate, landing protocol, errata location.

errata.note: Rule 9 gained a one-line pointer to ADR 0003 (Relay Landing Ceremony Tiers), which extends the landing protocol. Amendment made via decision record per rule 2; hash recomputed in the same commit.

Rule 6 gained an erratum-level note citing the W2-D6 scripted scan layer (scripts/public-safety-scan.py); script plus human review applies from 2026-07-05. Non-semantic: the rule already provided for this transition. Hash recomputed in the same commit.

entries.title: Synthetic Fixture Strategy
entries.role: The binding rules for all evaluation data: synthetic-by-construction, SYNTHETIC marking and marker-block schema, non-realistic identifiers, grammar-placeholder values, grammar-ID mapping, data-never-code, the Stage B hard-case seed list, and the two-stage landing rule (Stage A strategy only; Stage B fixture data after W2-D6 landing-mode scan).

entries.title: W3 — Health Vault and Health Profile Foundations (Phase Runway / Alignment Report)
entries.role: The W3 phase runway: the no-AI product spine (vault, draft profile, approved profile, user review before active context), deliverable sequence D1-D7, required decision records, non-goals, and the entry gate. Authorises future W3 briefs, not implementation.

entries.title: W3 Closure Record
entries.role: Seals W3, Health Vault and Health Profile Foundations, satisfying W3-D7: the six deliverable seals cited by commit with phase-level records noted; every deliverable assessed against the runway (including the D5 absorption disposition); verification at phase closure (218 tests, unmasked scan pass, three pins unchanged since first product code, governance/product boundary held through every landing); the nine future-owned pending stubs named and honestly carried; the incident log (wrong-workspace scare, two Downloads losses, stale relay refused, exit-masking slip caught by the suite); the honest-findings roll-up; the W4/W5 gate naming next-era draft material without reviewing or pre-deciding it; and the deferred list. W4 has not started and must open through its own runway and gate. Authorises nothing to be built.

entries.title: W3-D2 Closure Record
entries.role: Seals W3-D2, the engine spine: the published chain (ADR 0013 plus milestones 1-4) cited by commit, verification state at closure (105 tests, scan pass, registry and manifest untouched by product landings), honest findings carried forward (key-copy residual, cardinality leakage, weak-passphrase honesty, non-atomic write), and the full deferred list. Authorises nothing to be built; W3-D3 not started.

entries.title: W3-D3 Closure Record
entries.role: Seals W3-D3, the Health Profile object model: the published chain (M1 shapes, M2 sealed persistence) cited by commit, the seven accepted planning rulings restated as the deliverable decision anchor, verification state at closure (139 tests, scan pass, registry and manifest untouched by the milestones), honest findings carried forward (evidence-by-absence, truth-label persistence refusal, in-payload typing, no interval defaults, deferred persistence of transition-era shapes), and the full deferred list. States plainly that D3 is the object model, not a transition engine, and that W3-D4 has not started. Authorises nothing to be built.

entries.title: W3-D4 Closure Record
entries.role: Seals W3-D4, the transition engine: the published chain (M1 catalogue/matrix/validator, M2 applier with user-act gating, the pending-ledger cleanup) cited by commit, the planning-brief and in-flight rulings restated as the deliverable decision anchor, verification state at closure (180 tests, scan pass, registry and manifest untouched by the milestones, truth-label persistence refusal cross-checked live), honest findings carried forward (repository-scoped UserAct guarantees, events emitted not stored, truth labels unpersistable, clockless T5 marker, T1 dormant, T8 terminal), and the deferred list. States plainly that W3-D5 and later deliverables are unstarted. Authorises nothing to be built.

entries.title: W3-D5 Closure Record
entries.role: Seals W3-D5, local storage / residue rules, as a deliverable satisfied by absorption: the full ADR 0004 clause-by-clause mapping carried in the record itself with proof names and commit anchors (default-deny persistence tested on every operation incl. kill and at-scale; logs plaintext-free by structure; forbidden artifact classes by structure and absence; no exceptions taken; platform residual documented in ink; user copies handed to D6 as rights), two honest notes (single-configuration triviality; by-absence protections inherited at the surface era), the absorbed-not-skipped statement under the runway resequencing clause, and the forward gates (durable-ledger decision before the D6 brief; D6 as the next build arc). Authorises nothing to be built.

entries.title: W3-D6 Closure Record
entries.role: Seals W3-D6, export/backup/restore: the published chain (M1 backup and restore symmetry, M2 export-as-right) cited by commit; the seven planning rulings (headlined by the WBWB circularity correction: the key envelope rides reachable, never trapped inside what it unlocks) and the two M2 rulings restated; the consumed ADR 0015 inputs named; the WBWB container shape, five-step restore validation order, and export-right contract carried in ink at structural level; verification at closure; honest findings (in-memory assembly, uninventoriable-without-passphrase, never-silent sidecar, erasure cannot reach user-taken copies, torn-tail honesty travels); and the deferred list. States plainly that W3-D7 has not started. Authorises nothing to be built.

entries.title: W4 — Room Contracts (Phase Runway / Alignment Brief)
entries.role: The W4 phase runway: four room-contract jurisdictions (Wellness, Kitchen, Gym, Meditation); the six required doctrine records (DR-W4-01 through DR-W4-06); deliverable sequence W4-D1 through W4-D7; explicit non-goals; forward dependencies (W5 adapter isolation enforcement, W6 governed string catalogue) named and carried, not blocking; and the entry gate (three documents, three acceptances). Authorises future W4 briefs, not scaffolding and not implementation.
